name: Satyr
effect: Your party is transported to a lavish, enchanted feast, attended by any beings of the DM's choice. While at the feast, characters may not cast spells or draw weapons.

name: Stag
effect: Choose any one being to designate as your quarry. You always know the location and status of your quarry. When you kill it, you immediately gain a level.

name: Pestilence
effect: You are afflicted with a contagious, disfiguring disease. The symptoms and method of transmission are chosen by the DM.

name: Masque
effect: Your appearance is irrevocably changed, at the DM's discretion.

name: Harpy
effect: You or one or your companions is kidnapped by a harpy and flown to a distant shore.

name: Bastion
effect: For the next 24 hours, you are completely impervious to physical harm.

name: Legion
effect: You gain the service of a band of 20 level 1 soldiers. they follow your orders unflinchingly, and with complete loyalty.

name: Siren
effect: You capture the attention of a sorcerous maiden. She immediately begins planning to lure you to her lair and make you her thrall.

name: Dragon
effect: You encounter a hostile dragon.

name: Weaver
effect: Choose a character. You determine that character's final destiny. You do not know when this fate will come to pass, but it becomes certain and inescapable.

name: Shroud
effect: You instantly lose all memories or your past. You retain all skills, levels, and abilities. You may only recover your memories through a wish, or divine intervention.

name: Phantom
effect: This card comes to life as an unkillable, implacable specter. It will follow you wherever you go, and may pass through walls in order to do so.

name: Revenant
effect: Resurrect one dead character. One aspect of their personality is permanently changed.

name: Serpent
effect: One of your party members (chosen by the DM) is afflicted with a deadly, undetectable poison. Unless prevented with a wish, it will kill the character within one day.

name: Minotaur
effect: Your party is transported to the entrance of a labyrinth. The only escape is to find the center. A guardian minotaur lurks somewhere within.

name: Destrier
effect: You gain a magical steed chosen from among the following: bear, elk, stallion, rhino, sloth, or wolf.

name: Hound
effect: You gain the loyalty of a magical animal companion, chosen from among the following: boar, crow, hound, monkey, or serpent.

name: Orchard
effect: You gain the seed of a fruit tree. If you plant it, it grows to fruition in 7 days. Unless the tree is chopped down, it provides unlimited food and sustenance forever.

name: Mercian Wolves
effect: Your party is hunted by a pack of 16 wolves.

name: Covenant
effect:
A deity (of the DM's chooice) demands you complete a trial of its choosing.
If you succeed, it will grant nearly any request. If you fail, you forfeit your soul.

name: Tempest
effect: Your immediate vicinity is wracked by a terrible storm. If you are inside, the structure is destroyed entirely. Every character takes 2d10 damage.

name: Doppleganger
effect: This card comes to life as an identical version of you, including skills, memories, and equipment, except with the opposite alignment.

name: Djinn
effect: A djinn grants you audience with any one being. The djinn summons that being to your location for 5 minutes, then returns it where it came from.

name: Aperture
effect: An interdimensional portal appears, leading to any one place of your choosing. It remains open for one hour.

name: Titania
effect: A fae queen offers a trade: if you sacrifice something extraordinarily precious to you, you may look at the top 3 cards of this deck and choose a card to draw.

name: Scorpion
effect: Kill any one character, no matter who or where.

name: Spire
effect: Choose a skill. You gain +2 to all checks using that skill. You gain proficiency in this skill if you did not already have it. Draw Again.
